name: Washed / Wet Process
description: The cherry is completely removed from the seed before drying. This method highlights the intrinsic qualities of the bean (the terroir and variety) rather than the fruit surrounding it.
steps: Fermentation in water tanks (12-72 hrs) to remove mucilage
Drying on raised beds or patios
flavor_impact: Clean, bright, high acidity, articulate. The terroir and variety shine through with minimal fruit influence.

name: Natural / Dry Process
description: The oldest method. The coffee cherry is dried whole, intact with the seed inside. The seed absorbs sugars and fruit flavors from the cherry as it dries.
steps: Drying whole cherries on raised beds (2-4 weeks)
flavor_impact: Heavy body, intensely sweet, prominent fruit notes (often berry or jam), lower perceived acidity.

name: Honey / Pulped Natural
description: The skin is removed, but some amount of the sticky, sugary mucilage (the 'honey') is left on the bean while it dries.
flavor_impact: A middle ground. Sweeter and heavier body than washed, but cleaner with more acidity than naturals.

name: Anaerobic Fermentation
description: A modern, experimental method where coffee is fermented in a completely oxygen-free environment (sealed tanks), allowing different microbes to dominate the fermentation.
flavor_impact: Wildly unique, often yielding unexpected notes like cinnamon, bubblegum, lactic acidity (yogurt), or boozy/winey flavors.

name: Wet-Hulled / Giling Basah
description: Unique to Indonesia. The parchment shell is removed while the bean is still at a very high moisture content (25-50%), exposing the soft bean to the elements as it finishes drying.
flavor_impact: Dramatically reduces acidity, increases body immensely, and imparts distinctive earthy, woody, savory, or spicy notes.
